title: RNA Isolation from Cultured Mammalian Cells Using TRIzol Reagent
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"nplvb","text":"This protocol is intended to isolate total RNA from cultured mammalian cells using TRIzol reagent, facilitating downstream applications such as RT-PCR, Northern blotting, and gene expression analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"ujyox","text":"Always wear gloves, lab coat, and safety goggles when handling TRIzol, chloroform, and isopropanol. Work in a fume hood when using volatile organic solvents.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"tlsiv","text":"Materials - ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zeldm","text":"TRIzol Reagent 5 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k5ghc","text":"Chloroform 1.5 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ikwfo","text":"Isopropanol 1.2 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"sosw3","text":"Ethanol (75%) 1.5 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"qctp5","text":"RNAse-free Water 1 mL","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cda3h","text":"Microcentrifuge tubes 5 tubes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3uct0","text":"Pipette tips 5 tips","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ecug8","text":"Cell scraper 1","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"zo2gu","text":"Ice As needed","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"iknws","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3hy0s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5bytm","text":"Reagents - ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ue2aj","text":"TRIzol Reagent","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"joua2","text":"Chloroform","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cypjw","text":"Isopropanol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"eig8l","text":"Ethanol (75%)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Remove the culture medium from the cells using a vacuum or pipette. Add 1 mL of cold TRIzol reagent to the cell culture dish. Scrape the cells into the TRIzol using a sterile cell scraper. Transfer the cell suspension to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube on ice. Vortex the tube for 10 seconds to ensure complete lysis.
Add 0.2 mL of chloroform to the tube. Vortex for 15 seconds and let it sit at room temperature for 2-3 minutes. Centrifuge the tube at 12,000 g for 15 minutes at 4 °C.

Recover the upper aqueous phase (approximately 0.5 mL) into a new microcentrifuge tube. Add 1.25 mL of isopropanol to the aqueous phase. Invert the tube several times to mix. Incubate the tube at -20 °C for at least 1 hour to precipitate RNA.
Centrifuge the tube at 12,000 g for 30 minutes at 4 °C to pellet the RNA. Discard the supernatant and wash the RNA pellet with 1 mL of 75% ethanol. Centrifuge again at 7,500 g for 5 minutes at 4 °C.
creator: Anand Kumar Veeramachineni
